title: Dog Shivering but Not Cold: Causes and Triage Guide
meta_desc: Is your dog shivering but not cold? Learn about potential causes, urgency levels, and how to help your pet with our veterinary triage guide.
content_html:
TL;DR: Shivering in dogs when they aren't cold often signals pain, stress, or underlying medical issues like toxicity. Seek immediate veterinary care if the shivering is accompanied by vomiting, lethargy, or difficulty walking.

Why is my dog shivering or trembling when they aren't cold?

When a dog shivers or trembles despite being in a warm environment, it is usually a sign that their body is reacting to something other than temperature. In many cases, this involuntary muscle activity is a physical manifestation of pain, high stress, or an underlying metabolic or neurological issue. Unlike shivering for warmth, these tremors often stem from the nervous system or a reaction to internal discomfort.

Is my dog's shivering considered a veterinary emergency?

  • The urgency level for a dog shivering without a clear cause is Medium.
  • While it may be a reaction to excitement or minor nausea, it can also indicate serious conditions like toxin ingestion, hypoglycemia, or severe chronic pain.
  • Monitor your dog closely for any worsening symptoms.
  • Contact a veterinarian immediately if shivering is accompanied by vomiting, lethargy, or difficulty walking.

How can a photo or video help the veterinary triage process?

  • Capturing a video is one of the most helpful tools because dogs often stop shivering the moment they enter a stressful clinic environment.
  • A video provides a clear record of the behavior in a natural setting, helping the vet distinguish between localized tremors, generalized shivering, or focal seizures.
  • Providing visual evidence allows the veterinary team to provide a much faster and more accurate diagnosis.

Clinical Context (Merck Veterinary Manual)

Shivering in dogs, independent of cold exposure, can indicate underlying neurologic issues such as focal seizures or neuropathy, potentially requiring neurologic testing such as MRI and therapeutic trials involving medications like levetiracetam, potassium bromide, or phenobarbital. Furthermore, conditions like Holocyclotoxin paralysis can disrupt normal thermoregulation, leading to the absence of shivering even when hypothermia is present. Profound hypo- or hyperthermia may occur suddenly and can be easily misdiagnosed.

Chapter: Neurology, Behavioral

Source: The Merck Veterinary Manual, 11th Edition (Page 1562)

faqs.question: Can anxiety cause my dog to shiver?
faqs.answer: Yes, emotional distress or fear can cause significant trembling in dogs, often referred to as 'stress shivers'.

faqs.question: Is shivering a sign of pain in dogs?
faqs.answer: Yes, shivering is a very common non-verbal cue that a dog is experiencing physical pain, particularly in the back or abdomen.

faqs.question: What toxins cause dogs to shake?
faqs.answer: Ingesting items like chocolate, xylitol, or certain garden pesticides can cause muscle tremors and shivering as the body reacts to the poison.
